title: Umami French Mushroom Terrine
description: A light, indulgent terrine brimming with umami‑rich mushrooms, bound with ground poppy seeds and herbs, served chilled as a refreshing lunch dish.
instructions: Bring a pot of salted water to a gentle boil.
Add the whole mushrooms and blanch for 2 minutes, then drain and pat dry.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the blanched mushrooms, dried thyme, rosemary, sage, turmeric, salt, and pepper; sauté until the mushrooms release moisture and begin to caramelize, about 8–10 minutes.
Deglaze the pan with white wine, letting it reduce by half, then stir in soy sauce.
Transfer the mushroom mixture to a bowl and let cool slightly.
Whisk in ground poppy seeds and almond flour until a moist, cohesive batter forms.
Press the mixture firmly into a greased loaf pan or terrine mold, smoothing the top.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until firm.
Remove from the fridge, slice into rounds, and serve chilled, optionally garnished with fresh parsley.
